Spinosaurus wades through brown shallows, crocodilian snout submerged. Sudden lunge—silver fish thrashes in powerful jaws. Ripples spread outward through lily pads.
Massive Deinosuchus floats motionless in dark water, only nostrils and eye ridges visible. Dragonflies land briefly on its ancient, barnacle-encrusted skull.
Parasaurolophus herd drinks cautiously at river's edge, perfect reflections shimmering. Lead bull raises head, water streaming from its duck-billed mouth, alert.
Pteranodon skims low over glassy surface, beak cutting water. Fish explodes upward in spray. Wingtips touch ripples as it climbs with breakfast.
Argentinasaurus moves through flooded cypress forest, water reaching its massive belly. Trees part like grass. Displaced fish swirl in churning wake behind.
Weathered log spans murky channel. Compsognathus pack scurries across single-file, barely disturbing moss. One pauses midway to snap at passing dragonfly.
Dawn fog clings to swamp surface. Enormous shadows move slowly through gray mist. Bellowing calls echo across water as shapes materialize—feeding sauropods.
Ancient turtle colony basks on half-submerged log. Dragonflies with two-foot wingspans patrol humid air. Mosquitoes hum in thick, moisture-laden atmosphere.
Baryonyx stalks through knee-deep marsh, each step releasing methane bubbles. Suddenly freezes, watching fish school. Curved claw strikes lightning-fast through brown water.
Wide river delta sprawls under morning sun. Sandbars dot shallow channels. Maiasaura colony nests on muddy banks, guarding eggs from rising water.
Suchomimus stands statue-still in shallows, crocodile-like snout poised above surface. Fish dart below. Explosive sideways snap sends spray three feet high.
Sarcosuchus, forty-foot super-croc, lurks beneath duckweed blanket. Only eyes break green surface. Juvenile hadrosaur approaches shore, unaware of patient horror.
Triceratops herd crosses slow-moving river at shallow ford. Adults form protective circle while calves drink. Water reflects their frill silhouettes against sunset.
Quetzalcoatlus glides inches above water, shadow racing across surface. Beak dips, snatches fish without breaking stride. Massive wings never touch rippling river.
Diplodocus wades chest-deep through swollen waterway, long neck swaying above. Smaller fish feed frantically on parasites disturbed from its gray, wrinkled hide.
Fallen mangrove creates natural bridge over tannin-dark creek. Oviraptor crosses carefully, eggs cradled in arms. Water below swirls with hunting gar.
Morning mist drifts through cypress knees. Allosaurus silhouette emerges from white curtain, wading slowly. Water drips from belly. Hunting calls reverberate eerily.
Massive river turtle surfaces near bank, shell covered in smaller turtles. Giant dragonflies mate mid-flight above. Air thick enough to drink, heavy with life.
Carnotaurus splashes through marshland pursuing protoceratops. Mud sprays with each pounding step. Prey dodges through reeds as predator's reflection fragments in chocolate water.
Mississippi-wide river branches into delta channels. Sandbars crowd with nesting pterosaurs. Hadrosaur families claim elevated banks, building mud-mound nests above flood line.
Spinosaurus walks slowly through fog-shrouded shallows, sail cutting through mist like a shark fin. Kingfisher-sized birds scatter from half-submerged driftwood.
Kaprosuchus launches from murky water at drinking ornithomimid. Boar-like tusks gleam. Bird-mimic leaps backward, spray exploding. Ancient croc slides back, patient.
Edmontosaurus herd gathers at clear spring-fed pool within swamp. Reflections perfect in still water. Young ones play-splash while adults scan foggy perimeter.
Thalassodromeus swoops repeatedly over river, each pass collecting fish. Wingtips brush surface, leaving parallel lines. Beak pouch bulges with wriggling silver catch.
Camarasaurus family moves through flooded forest, water to their shoulders. Tree trunks disappear into brown depths. Wake attracts schools of opportunistic fish.
Ancient bald cypress trunk bridges stagnant channel. Microraptor glides down, lands gracefully, crosses on clawed feet. Pauses to drink, watching water striders.
Fog bank rolls across swamp at first light. Massive shapes materialize—Alamosaurus herd feeding on high vegetation. Water steams gently around pillar-like legs.
Half-submerged log crawls with prehistoric turtles, stacked three-deep. Dragonflies with falcon wingspans hunt above steaming water. Humidity condenses on every surface instantly.
Metriacanthosaurus moves through calf-deep marsh, head low, tracking. Stops abruptly—fish scattered ahead reveal prey's path. Explosive rush through reeds sprays mud.
River braids into channels around sandbars. Pteranodon colony nests on highest ground. Stegosaurus claims muddy bank territory, tail spikes gleaming wet near water.
Irritator, aptly named, fishes patiently in coffee-colored shallows. Long snout underwater, waiting. Sudden upward jerk—fish impaled on needle teeth, scales flashing.
Purussaurus, largest caiman ever, drifts like submerged log. Only bumpy eye ridges visible. Young allosaur drinks upstream, unaware death floats ten meters away.
Corythosaurus herd wades into river shallows, drinking deeply. Elaborate crests mirror perfectly in still water. Sentinel trumpets warning—reflections shatter as herd scatters.
Ornithocheirus patrol river in formation, wing-shadows racing across surface. Each dips rhythmically, snatching fish. Water droplets trail from beaks like scattered pearls.
Brachiosaurus stands shoulder-deep in wide river, neck extending to canopy. Small fish clean its submerged skin. Current flows around living island, patient and ancient.

Tupuxuara glides barely above river, shadow keeping pace on water. Beak penetrates surface without breaking flight. Fish secured, wingtips never touch, climbing away.

Fog thick as smoke engulfs mangrove channels. Massive splashing echoes invisibly through whiteness. Mist tears briefly—Argentinasaurus drinking—then heals, swallowing shape completely again.
Prehistoric mata mata surfaces slowly, shell ecosystem of moss and passengers. Dragonflies size of hawks circle territories. Humidity so thick it hazes vision, lungs labor.

Carnotaurus charges through swamp pursuing protoceratops, water exploding with each bound. Prey zigzags desperately through reeds. Bull-horned predator's reflection shatters repeatedly in chocolate water.
River delta fans into ocean in countless channels and sandbars. Pterodactylus rookery blankets highest ground. Maiasaura nests cluster elevated banks, guarding eggs from seasonal floods.
